nodes.label: Spaceflight Neurodegenerative Signature
nodes.description: Convergent neurodegenerative molecular signature identified across three brain cell types in spaceflight-exposed mice. GSEA reveals shared pathway activation with ALS, Parkinson's, Huntington's, and Alzheimer's disease.

nodes.label: Brainstem Neurons
nodes.description: Brainstem glutamatergic neurons with the strongest neurodegenerative pathway convergence in the dataset. All five major neurodegenerative disease KEGG pathways (ALS, Parkinson's, Huntington's, Alzheimer's, Prion) are significantly enriched — the defining result of this analysis. 3,655 DE genes between spaceflight and ground control, with oxidative phosphorylation disruption and ubiquitin-mediated proteolysis activation pointing to shared molecular mechanisms between microgravity exposure and neurodegeneration.

nodes.label: Oligodendrocytes
nodes.description: Myelin-producing cells showing severe metabolic collapse under spaceflight. Translation, mitochondrial ATP synthesis, and cellular respiration pathways are all downregulated.

nodes.label: CB Granule Neurons
nodes.description: Cerebellar granule neurons — the dominant brain cell population. Show disrupted glucose homeostasis and activated calcium stress signaling under spaceflight.

nodes.label: ALS
nodes.description: Motor neuron disease pathway. The most significantly enriched KEGG pathway in spaceflight brainstem neurons (p=1.04×10⁻¹²). Suggests spaceflight activates molecular machinery shared with ALS pathogenesis.

nodes.label: Parkinson's Disease
nodes.description: Dopaminergic neurodegeneration pathway. Enriched in spaceflight brainstem neurons (p=5.75×10⁻¹⁰). Involves mitochondrial dysfunction and oxidative stress — both confirmed by independent DE analysis.

nodes.label: Huntington's Disease
nodes.description: Polyglutamine repeat disorder pathway. Enriched in spaceflight brainstem neurons (p=1.22×10⁻⁷). Shares mitochondrial dysfunction and protein aggregation mechanisms with other neurodegenerative pathways.

nodes.label: Alzheimer's Disease
nodes.description: Amyloid and tau pathology pathway. Enriched in spaceflight brainstem neurons (p=2.67×10⁻⁷). Complement pathway suppression (C1qa, C1qb) in microglia further supports neuroinflammatory connection.

nodes.label: Oxidative Phosphorylation
nodes.description: Mitochondrial energy production pathway. Downregulated across multiple cell types. Confirmed by DE analysis showing Cox8a, Ndufs5, Ndufb7 (mitochondrial complex genes) as top ML features.

nodes.label: Translation
nodes.description: Protein synthesis machinery. Strongly downregulated in oligodendrocytes — the most negative NES score. Ribosomal proteins (Rpl21, Rpl18) also appear as top ML classifier features.

nodes.label: Mitochondrial ATP Synthesis
nodes.full_name: Mitochondrial ATP Synthesis Coupled Electron Transport (GO:0042775)
nodes.description: Mitochondrial electron transport chain. Downregulated in oligodendrocytes, consistent with the KEGG oxidative phosphorylation finding. Indicates energy crisis in myelin-producing cells.

nodes.label: Glucose Homeostasis
nodes.description: Blood sugar regulation pathway. Downregulated in cerebellar granule neurons. Combined with potassium ion transport disruption, suggests metabolic and electrophysiological dysfunction.

nodes.label: Calcineurin-NFAT Signaling
nodes.description: Calcium-dependent stress signaling. Upregulated in cerebellar granule neurons. Calcineurin-NFAT is activated by sustained calcium influx — a hallmark of neuronal stress and excitotoxicity.

nodes.label: Blood-Brain Barrier
nodes.description: BBB integrity maintenance. Downregulated in brainstem neurons. BBB disruption under spaceflight could allow peripheral immune factors to enter the brain, exacerbating neuroinflammation.

nodes.label: Apoptotic Regulation
nodes.full_name: Regulation of Cysteine-Type Endopeptidase Activity in Apoptotic Process (GO:0043281)
nodes.description: Programmed cell death regulation. Upregulated in brainstem neurons. Caspase activation indicates cells are entering apoptotic pathways — consistent with the neurodegenerative signature.

nodes.label: Radiation Response
nodes.description: Cellular response to ionizing radiation. Upregulated in cerebellar granule neurons. Directly relevant to spaceflight — cosmic radiation exposure on the ISS is a known hazard for astronaut health.

nodes.label: Ubiquitin Proteolysis
nodes.description: Protein degradation via ubiquitin-proteasome system. Upregulated in brainstem neurons (p=2.05×10⁻⁸). Cells are actively clearing damaged proteins — a hallmark of proteotoxic stress in neurodegeneration.
